notes: Codex /fork adaptation. Codex's Source is FilesUnderRoot(~/.codex/sessions): a proc-<PID> pre-session appears at launch, hands off to a rollout-keyed UUID-1 session on the first prompt, runs one turn, then /fork EAGERLY spawns a SECOND rollout (UUID-2) carrying a fresh session_id plus the replayed pre-fork history (its session_meta records forked_from_id=UUID-1, but the codex parser skips session_meta so the daemon sees UUID-2 as an independent fresh session, not a linked child). A turn on the forked thread then runs ready→working→ready on UUID-2 while UUID-1 stays frozen. The scenario-meanings 'linked new session_id if the adapter forks' is observable here only as v2_session_birth (new_session:true) — a rollout-keyed session_id never seen before. REGRESSION FIXTURE: moved here from scenarios/checkpoint-rewind, which is now agent_supports:no — codex /fork is a conversation fork, not a checkpoint rewind (no file-edit restore; see that cell's assessment). It is retained as a regression capture of /fork OBSERVABILITY. codex forks to a new session_id, and the original rollout's daemon SESSION ROW is removed via the #169 same-PID cleanup: both rollouts share one PID, so when UUID-2's PID is discovered the old session is deleted — transcript_removed for UUID-1 at seq 4939, ~7ms after the new pid_discovered, NOT a readyTTL sweep (the old rollout FILE meanwhile lingers frozen on disk). There is no old_session_ended phase: a scoping choice (v2_session_birth is the canonical observable), not because codex emits no removal. The driver's multi-session session.uuids capture BOTH rollouts, so the fixture contains UUID-1 and UUID-2 and validates 8/8.

text: Pre-session row (proc-<PID>) appears in ready within 1s of codex launch
After the first prompt lands, the rollout-keyed session appears in ready — proc-<PID> hands off to a fresh UUID
First user prompt drives ready → working on the rollout session
First turn completes back to ready (same rollout UUID), leaving an earlier checkpoint to fork from

Spec: 'a linked new session_id if the adapter forks' — /fork spawns a fresh rollout UUID never seen before, restored from the rewound point; this is the strongest observable proxy for 'the conversation was rewound and forked'
Next prompt after the rewind starts cleanly from the rewound point: drives the NEW forked session ready → working (must NOT match a stale working transition on UUID-1)
The post-rewind turn completes back to ready on the new forked session — clean ready → working → ready arc
